description:
Original design intent was heavier *implicit* use of the git log; the implementation drifted toward treating closed tasks as gone. Amendment: lean back in — treat the log as real, searchable content, always scanned MOST-RECENT-DOWN. Three concrete changes plus one unifying discipline:

1. Remove 'bl ready' entirely; replace with 'bl list --ready'. One listing verb, composable filters, fewer verbs (subtraction). --ready filters to the ready() predicate (live + unclaimed + claim-blockers resolved, §10) and carries ready's ordering (priority asc, absent last, then created asc). prime's returned ready-set shape is unaffected (data, not verb).
2. 'bl list' keeps defaulting to open (as today) but gains filters for reaching old/dead tasks: --closed / --all and date/range (and tag/text) filters, served from history. Closed tasks are not gone — they are older content.
3. 'bl show <id>' falls through to history on a live miss: live tasks/<id>.md first, else walk history newest->oldest and reconstruct the first hit.

UNIFYING DISCIPLINE — recency resolution. Every id->content lookup is: live file first, else most-recent-in-history, stop at first hit. This DISSOLVES the id-reuse-vs-history concern (was going to be a 'scan history to prevent reuse' ball — now unwanted): at most one incarnation is ever live (the filesystem guarantees it), and a reused id unambiguously means 'the current incarnation, or the most recent dead one.' The which-version problem is solved by construction; the 4-hex space stays fine; no widened ids, no extra collision check. Prevent-reuse was the wrong lever; recency-order resolution is the right one.

SUBTLETY to nail (not a blocker): delivered_in (§11, 'git log --grep [bl-id]') must bound its walk at the CURRENT incarnation's creation commit (most-recent --diff-filter=A of tasks/<id>.md) so a reused-but-undelivered id can't match a prior incarnation's delivery commit. Same most-recent-down rule applied to the grep.

Touches §2 (history-as-archive), §9 (read verbs), § id-generation (resolution discipline), §15 (revision-log entry). Design topic: attack before folding — keep open until it holds.
title: Topic: treat git log as content — recency-resolved id lookup; ready->list --ready; list history filters; show fallthrough
